availability of goods or services. When the is mo demand f a pro , suppliers m make i
more expe to incr profits. Conve , an exc supply can lead to price reductions.
Market equilibrium occurs when supply matches demand, resulting in stable prices. The real world,
however, is rarely as simple as this. Various factors influence these dynamics, including consumer
preferences, production costs, and external events.

questions.directive: Read an email.
questions.prompt: What can be inferred about Conference Room B?

passage.text:
Subject: Upcoming meeting
Hi Amy,
I'm afraid there has been another scheduling conflict. Conference
Room B is double booked on Friday morning. We can either meet
on Friday afternoon or move to the smaller Conference Room A.
However, Conference Room A does not have a projector. I am not
sure how you plan to present your ideas, so please let me know if
this would be a problem.
As we discussed, the meeting will focus on potential updates for
the campus Fall Festival. Please review the latest event schedule,
which is attached to this email. The university values student input
when planning campus events, so we greatly appreciate your
suggestions.
Thanks,
David
David Chen
University Administrator

questions.directive: Read a notice.
questions.prompt: On which day of the week will maintenance activities NOT take place?

passage.text:
Attention Residents of Maple Heights Dormitory
Subject: Scheduled Maintenance and Upgrades
Dear Residents,
Our maintenance team will be performing essential upgrades to the building's plumbing and electrical
systems. This work is necessary to ensure the continued safety and comfort of all residents. The
upgrades will take place over the course of three weeks, Monday. October 2, to Friday, October 20.
Key Details:
• Work Hours: Maintenance activities will occur between 9:00 A.M. and 5:00 P.M., Monday through
Friday.
•Water Interruptions: Temporary water outages may occur during this period. We will strive to keep
interruptions to a minimum and will notify you in advance.
• Noise Levels: Expect increased noise levels due to the nature of the work being performed.
•Access to Units: Maintenance personnel may need access to individual units to complete necessary
upgrades. Notices will be sent 24 hours in advance to inform you of any required access.
We appreciate your cooperation and understanding during this important maintenance period. If you
have any questions or concerns, please do not hesitate to contact the management office at 555-4321
or email us at management@mapleheights.com.
Sincerely.
Maple Heights Management

questions.directive: Read the passage and choose the best answer.
questions.prompt: According to the passage, value theory is concerned with all of the following EXCEPT

passage.text:
Value theory investigates the nature of values and the principles that determine what is worth

pursuing, both individually and collectively. Central questions include: What makes something good?

Are values objective features of the world or subjective preferences? A key distinction in value theory

is between intrinsic value, which denotes value something has in itself, and instrumental value, which

refers to the value of something as a means to an end.

One influential theory within this domain is utilitarianism, which posits that the moral worth of actions is

determined by their consequences, specifically the maximization of overall happiness or utility. This

consequentialist framework has shaped ethical reasoning in fields ranging from public policy to

economics. However, critics argue that utilitarianism's aggregation of happiness can negate individual

rights and seemingly justify morally troubling trade-offs. For example, allocating limited healthcare

resources to benefit the majority may disadvantage those with rare or costly conditions, raising

concerns about faimess and equity.

Contemporary value theorists often endorse value pluralism, the view that multiple, sometimes

conflicting values-such as autonomy, justice, and compassion are irreducibly important. This

pluralistic approach emphasizes the complexity of ethical decision-making and the need to navigate

competing goods instead of reducing them to a single metric.

Home Module1 - Router

passage.text:
The piano has a rich history that spans over 300 years, evolving from earlier keyboard instruments like the
clavichord and harpsichord. The mod piano w invented aro 1700 b Bartolomeo Cristofori, w
developed a n mechanism th made i possible f players t control the dynamics-soft and loud sounds-
by varying the pressure on the keys. Later key improvements include the addition of a cast iron frame and felt-
covered hammers, which allowed for greater volume and durability. These changes made the piano a central
instrument in both classical and popular music.

questions.directive: Read the passage and choose the best answer.
questions.prompt: The phrase "This field" in the passage refers to

passage.text: Green chemistry, an area of study within organic chemistry, aims to design products and processes that reduce or eliminate hazardous substances. This field promotes sustainable practices and renewable resources in chemical production. One breakthrough is biodegradable plastics. Traditional plastics are derived from petroleum, a non-renewable resource, and can take centuries to decompose. Biodegradable plastics, made from renewable materials like corn starch, can break down in months under composting conditions, reducing pollution and landfill burden. Another advancement is the use of catalysis to increase reaction efficiency. Catalysts speed up chemical reactions without being consumed and can often replace hazardous chemicals. Researchers have developed metal-organic frameworks (MOFs) that serve as efficient catalysts in various organic reactions. For example, they can be applied in the process of degrading of pollutants into harmless compounds, which can aid in water and air purification. In addition to catalysts, green chemistry also studies solvents. A solvent is a liquid that dissolves other substances and provides a medium for these substances to react. For example, using water as a solvent instead of organic solvents, which are often toxic, creates safer and more sustainable processes. Additionally, reactions in water can sometimes proceed faster than in organic solvents.
